title: Hosted Checkout Credential
description: Credential for hosted checkout escalation flows. Contains session reference and redirect URLs needed for the buyer's browser round-trip through the hosted payment UI. Unlike token_credential which carries payment tokens for processing, this credential carries no sensitive payment data — all payment handling occurs entirely within the hosted UI. Populated by the business in the Complete Checkout response.

type.description: Credential type discriminator. Identifies this as a hosted checkout credential.

session_id.description: Provider's session or order identifier for this hosted checkout instance. Examples: Razorpay order_id (order_AbCdEf123), PayPal token, Klarna session_id, Adyen sessionId. Used for: (1) correlating provider webhooks to the UCP checkout session, (2) debugging failed or abandoned checkouts, (3) cross-system support resolution. Optional — the continue_url and session polling are sufficient for the escalation flow without this field.

return_url.description: HTTPS URL where the hosted checkout UI redirects the buyer's browser after successful payment completion. The business MUST set this to a URL that allows the platform to regain control of the buyer session (e.g., a platform-provided callback endpoint or a business page that signals completion to the platform). Distinct from continue_url: continue_url takes the buyer TO the hosted checkout; return_url brings them BACK.

cancel_url.description: HTTPS URL where the hosted checkout UI redirects the buyer's browser if they abandon or cancel the payment. When absent, the provider's default behavior applies (typically closing the checkout or showing a retry option). Platforms SHOULD treat a redirect to this URL as a signal that the buyer abandoned checkout, but MUST still poll the checkout session for authoritative status.
